rows.plain_value:
When a requester submits an ARS request that triggers Preventive SOD, the requester can see the Risk Description in parentheses besides the Risk ID (which is typically not a user-friendly value).


However, when a manager, role owner, or other approver sees the same data, the Risk Description can only be displayed when the user hovers over the Information icon, which presents a negative user experience (especially when dealing with high volumes of SOD conflicts).


The ask is to make the experience consistent between requester and approver so that the Risk Description is always displayed without user needing to click or hover (and possibly even hide Risk ID if possible).


Please reference Fresh Desk ticket #1456102 Preventive SOD - Display SOD Risk Description for each line in ARS Confirmation page

rows.plain_value:
When a SOD violating access request is raised through Saviynt API, the business justification comes as Null even if the value is passed.

APICALL:{"requesttype":"NEW","username":"z_savtst4","endpoint":"E2D100_SAP ECC","securitysystem":"E2D100_SAP ECC","comments":"AccessNow Request ID","accountname":"z_savtst4","businessJustification":"Test","requestor":"Shubham.r","entitlement":[ {"entitlementtype":"saprole","entitlementvalue":"ZMFIN_AP_PAYMENT_PROG_XX_0000"}, {"entitlementtype":"saprole","entitlementvalue":"ZMFIN_RESET_CLRD_ITEMS_XX_0000"},
],"checksod":"true"}

As per the API documentation, for this {{url}}/ECM/{{path}}/createrequest API, Business justification field is not available.

Recommending the field to be added, so that business justification can be set instead of null.



-------------------------------


Adding additional context: Customer is using ServiceNow and the issue is occurring once requests are approved in SNOW and the API is invoked. The current API documentation for {{url}}/ECM/{{path}}/createrequest, does not call out a parameter for passing business justification. However, the request UI allows for entering a business justification. Based on the ability within the UI to enter a business justification, the customer is requesting an update to the API to pass that information back to SNOW as well.

rows.plain_value:
As of now, people are using the New Experience for many applications. However, for SAP applications that are connected to SAP GRC for External SOD Evaluation, it is required to switch to Classic UI.

The impact is that End Users are mislead with various interfaces based on the application to request, and it requires additional clicks to add a new account for a SAP application, which is not convenient as per UX standard.

Aditya Vikram
• Roles, Rules & Policies
rows.rich_value: • Roles, Rules & Policies

rows.plain_value:
1. Inactive applications should not generate tasks. If the application is not fully offboarded i.e. removed from the user update rule and actionable analytics, it continues to generate arstasks that get queued up on pending tasks and incrementally impact provisioning and performance
2. Unlike CERTIFICATION AND ANALYTICS_V2, Source ID is currently missing in the arstasks SOURCE column for task source via PROVRULE.
3. Adding a sourceID as the hanarulekey will help in swift identification and detective cleanup i.e. applicaiton offboarding.
4. Lack of arstaskkey for arstasks with source=PROVRULE has been operationally cumbersome while migrating apps from 6.5 > EIC.
rows.html_value:
1. Inactive applications should not generate tasks. If the application is not fully offboarded i.e. removed from the user update rule and actionable analytics, it continues to generate arstasks that get queued up on pending tasks and incrementally impact provisioning and performance

  2. Unlike CERTIFICATION AND ANALYTICS_V2, Source ID is currently missing in the arstasks SOURCE column for task source via PROVRULE.

  3. Adding a sourceID as the hanarulekey will help in swift identification and detective cleanup i.e. applicaiton offboarding.

  4. Lack of arstaskkey for arstasks with source=PROVRULE has been operationally cumbersome while migrating apps from 6.5 > EIC.

cnedelcu@us.ibm.com Jan 19, 2024
Hi Product Team, is this idea indeed confirmed for release 24.1? will it work in current UI and in Gen2UI or only in Gen2UI? Thank you

rvartak@simeio.com Jan 19, 2024
https://docs.saviyntcloud.com/bundle/Release-Notes/page/Content/v24x/Release-Notes-24-1.htm


It does not matter which UI is used its admin function


Efficient Management of Access Assigned Through Rules

When tasks are generated through technical or user update rules, the SOURCE column in the ARSTASKStable now accurately indicates the origin of these tasks. Specifically, if a rule is a birthright rule, the SOURCE will be displayed as ZERODAY. Conversely, for non-birthright rules, the source will be indicated as PROVRULE. This refined presentation of sources equips administrators with enhanced troubleshooting and auditing capabilities for accesses assigned through rules. For more information, see Creating User Update Rules (https://docs.saviyntcloud.com/csh?topicname=Creating-User-Update-Rules&pubname=EIC-Admin-v24x) and Creating Technical Rules (https://docs.saviyntcloud.com/csh?topicname=Creating-Technical-Rules&pubname=EIC-Admin-v24x) in the Enterprise Identity Cloud Administration Guide.

cnedelcu@us.ibm.com Nov 25, 2024
Thank you Product team. We'll test and come back in case of questions.


(internal customer tracking, AHR-476, CL, CEN)

Thank you

hachung@coupang.com Jan 18, 2024

rvartak@simeio.com Jan 18, 2024

It has been fixed in v24,1



cnedelcu@us.ibm.com Jan 19, 2024

Hi Product Team, is this idea indeed confirmed for release 24.1? will it work in current UI and in Gen2UI or only in Gen2UI? Thank you



rvartak@simeio.com Jan 19, 2024

https://docs.saviyntcloud.com/bundle/Release-Notes/page/Content/v24x/Release-Notes-24-1.htm


It does not matter which UI is used its admin function


Efficient Management of Access Assigned Through Rules

When tasks are generated through technical or user update rules, the SOURCE column in the ARSTASKStable now accurately indicates the origin of these tasks. Specifically, if a rule is a birthright rule, the SOURCE will be displayed as ZERODAY. Conversely, for non-birthright rules, the source will be indicated as PROVRULE. This refined presentation of sources equips administrators with enhanced troubleshooting and auditing capabilities for accesses assigned through rules. For more information, see Creating User Update Rules and Creating Technical Rules in the Enterprise Identity Cloud Administration Guide.




cnedelcu@us.ibm.com Nov 25, 2024

Thank you Product team. We'll test and come back in case of questions.


(internal customer tracking, AHR-476, CL, CEN)



PM Team (Contributor) Jan 20, 2025

Thanks for submitting the idea, agree that source ID is not populated but ASSIGNEDFROMRULE column can be used for the same purpose.



simone.espanhol@ibm.com Jan 31, 2025

Hello Product Team,

I see th status changed to 'Future Release'.

Any particular information of which Future Release it will be assigned this IDEA implementation?

Thanks! (AHR-476,SE)



PM Team (Contributor) Feb 24, 2025

Hello @guest Did you get chance to look into the previous comment? Can you please use ASSIGNEDFROMRULE column instead of SOURCE ID?



cnedelcu@us.ibm.com Mar 3, 2025

The team will update their feedback soon, thank you! (AHR-476, CEN)



cnedelcu@us.ibm.com Mar 6, 2025

Hi Product team


Could you please help clarify Why is there a need for SourceID?

There's already ASSIGNEDFROMRULE column that specifies the rule.

For example, this is the screenshot from 'Pending Tasks on AccessHub Parent' report where that column is already used and we're getting the rule name in the TASK_SOURCE DETAILS column.



Thank you

(AHR-476, CEN and Jan)




PM Team (Contributor) Mar 12, 2025

@guest Hello, this Idea ask is to have SOURCEID field populated in ARSTASKS table. We have given alternate solution of using ASSIGNEDFROMRULE which will have rulekey and can be utilized to get rule name.



Manish Acharya Apr 21, 2025

Closing this idea as the alternate solution has been provided



cnedelcu@us.ibm.com Apr 22, 2025

Thank you Product team.


We can get the required information from the ASSIGNEDFROMRULE column.

Therefore, we can close this Idea.

rows.plain_value:
The WINCONNECTOR connection type should support configuration using a placeholder JSON similar to a REST Connector (ConfigJSON) to allow settings such as provisioningLimit, showLogs, and connectionTimeoutConfig.

This provides greater flexibility for troubleshooting and ensures the connection can be closed gracefully, allowing other activities to proceed without interruption. We are seeing jobs getting stuck, running for a longer time with the WS retry job configured with WINCONNECTOR connection type.

Currently Web Application Firewall limit for Saviynt EIC is 5000/IP/5minutes.

We heard , this limit ensures that the network traffic communicating with EIC is legitimate, and enhances security.

This limit is too small to implement.
The best hope is that there is no limit, but I would like the limit to be changed to 10,000, 30,000, etc.

rows.plain_value:
If user gets assigned new accesses, when sending this info as a rest patch request Saviynt should also send a list of users already existing entitlements.

So, e.g.
User gets access 'abc' in the new access requestUser already has accesses 'xyz' and '123'
All of these (abc, xyz, 123) should be sent to the target system from saviynt at the same time.

The ticket raised for this issue is:

https://saviynt.freshdesk.com/support/tickets/1511845

The forum is also created while back on this issue:

https://forums.saviynt.com/t5/identity-governance/send-existing-entitlements-during-new-access-request-rest/m-p/14854#M5665

rows.plain_value:
Hi Team,

As of now, for the SAP connector, the SNC certificate should be uploaded to Saviynt only from the backend. We dont have any option to upload the SNC certificate from UI. They have to raise a devops jira to upload the file from the backend. Please do the needful to make the SNC file upload self-service.

rows.html_value:
idpath in rest connector should support string operations to handle hierarchical entitlement assignments.


I have a rest app where entitlement is actually a combination of 2 attributes in the response (name || resource). I'm able to combine the attribute values and assign it to entitlementID and entitlement_value but the mapping between the account and entitlement is not happening. I have put the id path same as the logic in entitlementID but CP31 of accounts table is not getting populated due to which the relationship between account and entitlement is not established. Appreciate the assistance to resolve the mapping issue.

serviceRole1 || microservicename1

serviceRole1 || microservicename2

serviceRole2 || microservicename1

serviceRole2 || microservicename2

API Response

{
	"results":[
	{
            "user": {
                "userId": "b9ecf1f17a6f",
                "idpId": "mydomain.com",
                "email": "myuser@mydomain.com",
                "username": "myuser@domain.com"
            },
            "serviceRoles": [
                {
                    "serviceRoles": [
                        {
                            "name": "serviceRole1",
                            "membershipType": "DIRECT",
                            "resource": "microservicename1"
                        },
                        {
                            "name": "serviceRole1",
                            "membershipType": "DIRECT",
                            "resource": "microservicename2"
                        },
                        {
                            "name": "serviceRole2",
                            "membershipType": "DIRECT",
                            "resource": "microservicename1"
                        },
                        {
                            "name": "serviceRole2",
                            "membershipType": "DIRECT",
                            "resource": "microservicename2"
                        }
                    ]
                }
            ],
            "customRoles": []
        }
	]}

ImportAccountEntJson

{
  "accountParams": {
    "connection": "acctAuth",
    "processingType": "SequentialAndIterative",
    "statusAndThresholdConfig": {
      "accountsNotInImportAction": "Suspend",
      "accountThresholdValue": 1000
    },
    "call": {
      "call1": {
        "callOrder": 0,
        "stageNumber": 0,
        "http": {
          "url": "https://myapp.mydomain.com/api/users?pageStart=0&pageLimit=200",
          "httpContentType": "application/json",
          "httpMethod": "GET",
          "httpHeaders": {
            "Accept": "application/json",
            "token": "${access_token}"
          }
        },
        "listField": "results",
        "keyField": "accountID",
        "colsToPropsMap": {
          "accountID": "user.userId~#~char",
          "name": "user.email~#~char",
          "customproperty1": "user.idpId~#~char",
          "description": "user.username~#~char",
          "customproperty31": "STORE#ACC#ENT#MAPPINGINFO~#~char"
        },
        "pagination": {
          "nextUrl": {
            "nextUrlPath": "${response?.completeResponseMap?.nextLink==null?null:('https://myapp.mydomain.com'+response.completeResponseMap.nextLink)}"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "acctEntMappings": {
      "Service Roles": {
        "importAsEntitlement": true,
        "listPath": "serviceRoles[0].serviceRoles",
        "idPath": "#CONST#${String d1 = response.name; String d2 = response.resource; ret = d1 + \" || \" + d2; if(d2.equals(null)){return d1} else{return ret}}",
        "keyField": "entitlementID",
        "colsToPropsMap": {
          "entitlementID": "#CONST#${String d1 = response.name; String d2 = response.resource; ret = d1 + \" || \" + d2; if(d2.equals(null)){return d1} else{return ret}}~#~char",
          "entitlement_value": "#CONST#${String d1 = response.name; String d2 = response.resource; ret = d1 + \" || \" + d2; if(d2.equals(null)){return d1} else{return ret}}~#~char",
          "customproperty1": "name~#~char",
          "customproperty2": "resource~#~char"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "entitlementParams": {
    "connection": "acctAuth",
    "processingType": "SequentialAndIterative",
    "entTypes": {
      "Service Roles": {}
    }
  },
  "acctEntParams": {
    "processingType": "acctToEntMapping"
  }}

-Siva

rows.plain_value: Add filter to Azure AD account import
rows.html_value: Add filter to Azure AD account import
rows.rich_value: Add filter to Azure AD account import

rows.plain_value:
David Fannon Nov 29, 2022
Several customers are asking for this include SLB and Baker

Rajiv Kumar Jan 24, 2023
Hi Ivana,

Azure AD connector supports filtering accounts during import. Refer https://docs.saviyntcloud.com/bundle/AzureAD-v231/page/Content/Configuring-the-Integration-for-Account-and-Group-Import.htm#Filterin.

If your filtering requirements are not met with the current capability, do provide more details on the ask.

ivana.trginova@sk.ibm.com Jan 30, 2023
Hi Rajiv, i read the documentation and it sounds like the account import allows filter based on user attributes, but what we need is the accounts to be imported from specific groups. But the documentation says to use the ent import only will full account import. This is the ask: connector to allow account import based on filtered entitlement import. For example i have 1000 AD users spread across 20 AD groups, In group A i have 3 users, in group B i have 2 users. What i want to do is to import only the 5 users having access to group A and B, not all the 1000 users with their access to the 20 groups. Please let me know if that is possible. Thank you

Rajiv Kumar May 9, 2024
We reviewed the Azure AD API's and have not found one that can can filter the data based on group membership.

This idea will be closed.

rows.plain_value:
Hello Team,

For The REST connector when we try to establish the connectivity with Direct SSL Certficate it's not working.When we try to run the account or access import it was failing with 401.We have installed certficate under under java keystore and append the parmameters in the connection.if we innovke the certficate with curl command in server directly with differnt location insted of keystore we are geeting the all the target data in server.Up on checking with engiinreing team they confomred REST connector not spported to establish the connection with Direct SSL certficate.Could you please consider this enhancement and please provide the capbaliuty to estibalsh the Direct SSL Certicate connectivity for REST connector.

Curl example i have used to fetech target data directly from saviynt server.

Need to go certfiate location from server:

curl -k --tlsv1.2 --verbose --cert ./xxxxx.pem https://xxxxxxx/grc/ext/custom/ets/provisioning/accesses

rows.plain_value:
Manish  Acharya Dec 8, 2022
We will need correct description and requirement on this idea. We also need to check if this currently works.

Rajiv Kumar Jan 24, 2023
@Manish Acharya (https://saviynt-product.aha.io/users/7143927793708447237) @Anurag Panda (https://saviynt-product.aha.io/users/7065295865284108970) - I could only find https://docs.saviyntcloud.com/bundle/REST-v2021x/page/Content/Developers-Handbook.htm --> "SSL Parameter" describing about using the ssl parameter, but that too does not detail about using certificate for authentication. Can you point me to the section where we have outlined the steps? If not, let's get it added to the doc and use it to close this ask.

Rajiv Kumar Mar 23, 2023
@Vijay Kumar Avvaru (https://saviynt-product.aha.io/users/7208140955107235325) - The REST connector already supports certificate based authentication. You'll need to provide the details of the pem key file as a part of the ssl parameters. I'd suggest you to take this to the connector's triage team. @Anurag Panda (https://saviynt-product.aha.io/users/7065295865284108970) should be able to provide more details.

I am accepting this idea and linking it for changes to be done on EIC and doc update.

rows.html_value:
In case of an API response having unique identifier embedded in different fields in an array, we need to make use of both fields and based on that map entitlements. Example can be shown on BlackLine API response:

{ "items": [ { "product": { "id": "A", "name": "Account" }, "role": { "id": 12, "name": "Local Admin" } }, { "product": { "id": "M", "name": "Matching" }, "role": { "id": 12, "name": "Local Admin" } } ], "totalItems": 2}

In case that there is no access "Matching" for specific account on target, then response would look like this:

{ "items": [ { "product": { "id": "A", "name": "Account" }, "role": { "id": 12, "name": "Local Admin" } } ], "totalItems": 1}

Note that different "roles" will have corresponding "name" field populated based on type on target. Example "roles" can be: Preparer, Approver, Auditor, Local Admin, etc.. Example "names" can be: Account, Matching, etc..

Single mapping field wont generate unique identifier and thus cannot be mapped correctly.

Single mapping field wont generate unique identifier and thus cannot be mapped correctly.

rushikesh.vartak@pronoesis.com May 14, 2025
Merged idea - EIC-I-3729

Resolution comment from the closed idea below.

This is already supported as part of the Access Import for REST Connector. Below is the usage under colstoPropsMap for entitlementParams -

"colsToPropsMap": {
 "entitlementID": "#CONST#${return (response?.items?.serviceName == null || response?.items?.teams?.size() < 1) ? '' : response?.items?.serviceName + '_' + response?.items?.teams?[0]?.name;}~#~char",
 "entitlement_value": "#CONST#${return (response?.items?.serviceName == null || response?.items?.teams?.size() < 1) ? '' : response?.items?.serviceName + '_' + response?.items?.teams?[0]?.name;}~#~char",
 },


 this is solution for entitlementParams and ask in idea was acctEntParams  I have updated idea ticket.


https://forums.saviynt.com/t5/the-all-star-lounge/idea-merged-idea/m-p/167652#M569
https://saviyntsupport.saviynt.com/support/tickets/2082043

Regards,
Rushikesh Vartak

mbratink@sk.ibm.com Jul 26, 2023
Hello Team,

what is the status or an ETA for this idea? It's currently preventing us from automating 2 of our applications. It has been open for some time now and our app teams are getting impatient as it's causing a lot of manual work at the moment.

peter.hofer@bp.com Aug 8, 2023
Hi,


Not sure who posted this, however we a similiar constraint with Blackline. Our approach though was to strongly request that Blackline implement SCIM compliant api's to manage accounts and entitlements.


It would be beneficial if you could push them to do the same thing as at the moment they feel their API's are 'loved' by their customers, but in actual fact they are driving in a lot of cost.


Making it scim compliant would benefit all customers as most IDP's also support SCIM compliant connections so this wouldn't be limited to just Saviynt or customers with IGA.


Happy to chat furhter


Peter Hofer

While hte IGA vendor can solve it, lets make the world better by pushing the software vendor to implement SCIM, which is what they should be doing in a standards based world. They are driving cost in to all our businesses.

*Rant over ;-)*

rows.plain_value:
Problem Statement: AWS connector is adding root user as a principal in the trust relationship while provisioning AWS role to an IAM user.


Expected Behavior: It will be a more secure practice to add the individual IAM users as the principal in the role's trust relationship.


Freshdesk Ticket : https://saviynt.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/1592467
rows.html_value:
Problem Statement: AWS connector is adding root user as a principal in the trust relationship while provisioning AWS role to an IAM user.


Expected Behavior: It will be a more secure practice to add the individual IAM users as the principal in the role's trust relationship.


Freshdesk Ticket : https://saviynt.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/1592467
rows.rich_value:
Problem Statement: AWS connector is adding root user as a principal in the trust relationship while provisioning AWS role to an IAM user.


Expected Behavior: It will be a more secure practice to add the individual IAM users as the principal in the role's trust relationship.


Freshdesk Ticket : https://saviynt.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/1592467

rows.plain_value: On every screen there should be a 'Back' button to the main page you are on. For example, if I am on the 'Users' page and I click into a user to view their details, I want to have a Back button that I can click to easily go back to the full list of users. This should work the same on every screen, including roles, entitlements, accounts, everything. This will make it much simpler than going back using the hamburger icon or the web browser back button.

rows.plain_value: Most of the search screens does not provide the ability to get back to the search result screen if I click for details of that screen. For example when we search on User screen and click on details of one user, there is no way to get back to the previous screen that displays the search result. Also there is no one click to get back to the search screen to search another user. Browser back button does not work properly plus gives error which takes you back to Home screen specially when the session expires.

For example when we search on User screen and click on details of one user, there is no way to get back to the previous screen that displays the search result. Also there is no one click to get back to the search screen to search another user. Also there is no one click to get back to the search screen to search another user. Browser back button does not work properly plus gives error which takes you back to Home screen specially when the session expires.

Ehud Amiri Oct 16, 2024
Thank you for submitting this idea!
We're actively looking into it, and it aligns well with our broader direction around Non-Human Identity management. In this approach, a machine identity may have one or more associated accounts, such as service accounts, which fits the use cases you've described. We've already started direct conversations about this topic and will continue to engage as we explore the best solutions.

In the meantime, we encourage others to vote or share their thoughts and comments to help us prioritize this idea. Your feedback is critical as we continue shaping our product strategy!

Regards,
Ehud from the Product team

A decision was made to include this feature in a future release. This enhancement will be done as part of a comprehensive redesign of the service account management user experience. While the final timeline has not been determined, we are committed to delivering this enhancement as early as possible.

rows.plain_value:
We need a monitoring mechanism which provides insight into health of system on daily basis. We are looking for two monitoring components


Infra Monitoring

1. Long response times on API calls (datadog does the avg response time and alerts long running calls)
2. P95 latency (datadog does the avg response times on transactions and alerts slow response calls)
3. Any DB queries running long
4. Database infra utilization is over 75%


System Monitoring

1. Any scheduled jobs (rcon, reports, attestation, email, task etc) not kicked off for any system failure so that team can run it manually. A general flag in dashboard regarding these event will provide team to go and check specific failure on task/reports/email etc
2. Provisioning jobs not picking up pending tasks. A general flag in dashboard regarding these event will provide team to go and check why task created and not prcoessed by wsretry
3. Outbound Emails (failures) not being sent by system (approval, reports, notification etc). Dashboard or similar capability to display if there is a such failure in the last 24 hours (duration to be configurable) so that team can look into specific failures
4. Inbound Emails (failures) for approval not being processed by system (approval, reports, notification etc). Dashboard or similar capability to display if there is a such failure in the last 24 hours (duration to be configurable) so that team can look into specific failures
5. Log in service failure (bad gateway 502 error)
6. Connection failure or time out error. This will help us understand how much network is causing problem with provisioning. Example of recon job being failed because of network issues and provisioning job not being processed because of connection refusal
7. A log containing all critical errors (daily file) at system level so that we review what errors were logged by system e.g javs script error experienced by end users, login failures, adsi throwing task related errors. Also this will help dealing with new issues
8. If a recon job is stuck, it should be flagged. Azure take 1-2 hours but when it is stuck, it takes 24 hours before showing status of failure.

  2. Provisioning jobs not picking up pending tasks. A general flag in dashboard regarding these event will provide team to go and check why task created and not prcoessed by wsretry

  3. Outbound Emails (failures) not being sent by system (approval, reports, notification etc). Dashboard or similar capability to display if there is a such failure in the last 24 hours (duration to be configurable) so that team can look into specific failures

  6. Connection failure or time out error. This will help us understand how much network is causing problem with provisioning. Example of recon job being failed because of network issues and provisioning job not being processed because of connection refusal

  7. A log containing all critical errors (daily file) at system level so that we review what errors were logged by system e.g javs script error experienced by end users, login failures, adsi throwing task related errors. Also this will help dealing with new issues

  8. If a recon job is stuck, it should be flagged. Azure take 1-2 hours but when it is stuck, it takes 24 hours before showing status of failure.

  2. Provisioning jobs not picking up pending tasks. A general flag in dashboard regarding these event will provide team to go and check why task created and not prcoessed by wsretry

rows.plain_value:
Currently many of the fundamental components that are deployed to support our product are end of life. When a product is end of life, we are not able to get patches or vendor support to resolve issues. In the case of an ongoing critsit for Cerner, we had a 4 year old community version of NGINX that appears to be bottleneck for customer performance. We have no support through the vendor for NGINX (F5) nor through Azure (Microsoft) to analyze network performance of the AKS cluster. Ensuring we at least have components in support will allow us to bridge security gaps and have better performing components.

Once we do get up to date from the tech debt, we do need to stay in a cadence. For example AKS is only good for 12-15 months before it needs to be upgraded again. Clear refresh cycles need ot be defined to stay compliant.

Non-exhaustive list as of 5/12/2025

| Components | Latest EIC Version* | Latest Vendor Version |
| Elastic | 6.x | 8.x |
| Logstash | 6.x | 8.x |
| LogViewer ES | 7.x | 8.x |
| RabbitMQ |  |  |
| Nginx | 1.19.10  | 1.26.3 |
| Corretto Java | 8/11/17 (new services) | 21 - LTS |
| Tomcat | 9.x | 11.x |
| Grails | ecm - 2.3.11 | 6.2.3 |
| Spring Framework | ECM - 3 | 6.2.4 |
| Spring Boot | ECM - 2.7 | 3.4.3 |
| Spring Security |  | 6.4.3 |
| Jquery  | 1.12.4 | 3.7.1 |
| Jquery UI | 1.10.4 | 1.14.1 |
| moment.js | 2.0.0 | 2.30.1 |
| ckeditor.js | 4.0.1 | 5 (44.3.0) |
| Bootstrap |  | 5.3.3 |
| Nodejs | 14.21.3 | 22.14.0 |
| React | 18.2.0 | 19.0.0 |
| Ubuntu | 18 | 24.04.02 LTS |
| Ubuntu | 16 | 24.04.02 LTS |
| Centos  | 7 | N/A: EOL |
| Centos  | 8 (SC2 Client) | N/A: EOL |
| RHEL  | 7 | 9.5 |

rows.html_value: Gathering Feedback
Jitendra Sharma
• Segregation of Duty
rows.rich_value: • Segregation of Duty

rows.plain_value:
Currently the AAG module identifies all Oracle users with the access to commit a segregation of duties violation based on their system permissions; however, not all users with conflicts actively use the responsibilities that are assigned to them that cause these conflicts. For increased risk awareness and visibility, we would like to import the Oracle EBS Usage History (also called Sign On Audit) to determine if a user navigated to a responsibility and form related to both Activities/SOD Functions. This way we can flag an SOD conflict as either Potential (haven't accessed both Functions) or Actual (has accessed both Functions).

In Oracle (for Baker Hughes), we have turned on two types of tracking: 1) Page Access Tracking, and 2) Sign On Audit for all Users, Responsibilities, and Forms. The first tells the reviewer what OAF/HTML pages the user opened, and the second shows which Forms the user opened (but not necessarily the function/permission in question); in both cases, it logs which responsibility was used in each navigation but it does not track before or after values of the change (this is covered by limited Audit Trail functionality that is outside the scope of this ER). This only tells the reviewer that the user accessed the page/form but we don't know what the user did with it (view, create, update, or delete).

Here is a single set of tables that can be used to extract this data from the EBS database:

1. Primary Table: APPS.jtf\_pf\_ses\_activity

#1 Joins with APPS.fnd\_user based on userid

#1 joins with APPS..fnd\_responsibility\_vl based on respid

the JTF PageName then joins either with APPS.fnd\_form\_vl (form\_ID) if the JTF Tech Stack is 'FORM' or it holds the page name (no join).

The Form ID from FND\_FORM\_VL then joins with FND\_FORM\_FUNCTIONS\_VL based on Form ID to identify the EBS function (which is part of the SOD Function - Entitlement mapping in AAG).

This is a complicated use case so please let me know if a working session is in order or if you just need more info.

rows.plain_value:
Intermittent connections with Data Import for a single Oracle endpoint (called NEO), where the Data Import runs successfully every 3-5 attempts, but fails most of the time. We confirmed the connection test was successful and confirmed that JDBC connection string with the DBA's. However, our DBA recommended the following two based on his observations:


1. Row PreFetch size and latency issues due to geography
that fetch size when extracting data from database should be adjusted to sustain latency between the Saviynt instance (that I guess is on US) and source DB that is in EMEA.As you see session is always on "SQL*Net Message from client" with 0 seconds in wait (Event and Second in wait column) that means that application is asking Oracle DB only few rows per fetch from the selected result set of the query.Indeed the session in 31 minutes of connection at time I took the screenshot, spent 100 milliseconds in query and 189k milleconds waiting on data transfer. In other apps having similar problem, this was fixed changing the RowPrefetch to a value like 1000 (from default value that should be something like 10).


2. Closing session once data import is complete


Session Saviynt created on DB has been left there in NEO not closed after last entity imported (profile). That's another practice to be fixed to properly handle connection to database

Rajiv Kumar Jan 24, 2025
Greetings,

* W.r.t. the latency issue, you can set the connectionproperties of the db connector to update the defaulrRowPrefetchsize. See example below

{"defaultRowPrefetch":"100"}

* For the issue around the session closure, an internal issue has been created for triaging and will be handled accordingly.

This idea will be closed.

rows.plain_value:
Saviynt does not support the capability to translate IP addresses (NAT Gateway) to external customers on Transit Gateway connection types. Our goal is to pivot to a Transit Gateway connection type when we exit our data centers.

The absence of a NAT gateway means we have to provide Saviynt with customer IP Addresses to set up the tenants.

Today we provide Saviynt with 4 subnets with 255 hosts each for their use, but we would like to reclaim them and request an enhancement to support NAT Gateway services ideally before we transition to the Transit Gateway connection type.

The absence of a NAT gateway means we have to provide Saviynt with customer IP Addresses to set up the tenants.

Today we provide Saviynt with 4 subnets with 255 hosts each for their use, but we would like to reclaim them and request an enhancement to support NAT Gateway services ideally before we transition to the Transit Gateway connection type.

rows.plain_value:
Import details on which responsibilities can execute which programs through an assigned request group.


New Entitlement Types:

1. OEBS-Request-Group - pull from APPS.FND_REQUEST_GROUPS2. OEBS-Concurrent-Program - pull from APPS.FND_CONCURRENT_PROGRAMS_VL
Entitlement Associations: 1. OEBS-Responsibility to OEBS-Request-Group - use APPS.FND_RESPONSIBILITY_VL to find Request_Group_ID for each responsibility (a responsibility can only have one request group, and not all responsibilities have a request group assigned


2. OEBS-Request-Group to OEBS-Concurrent Program - use APPS. FND_REQUEST_GROUP_UNITS to find linkage between Request_Group_ID and Unit_Application_ID (joins with FND_CONCURRENT_PROGRAMS_VL.Concurrent_Program_ID)

rows.plain_value:
Hello Team,
We have a requirement to protect some accounts and access directly in the connection level from disabling and deleting the access. We have updated the connection commands (UPDATE_ACCOUNT_COMMAND,REMOVE_ACCESS_COMMAND ) as per the ask and it's working as expected but the problem is it's not printing the expected messages in PROVISIONINGCOMMENTS which leads to confusion to end-user as they don't know the reason for failure. we have tried all the possibilities like standard out (>&1) standard error (>&2) but it was printing the expected message.
for update account, it's just printing
Error in updating account:v08311
for remove access, nothing was printing just null.

Fresh Desk Ticket # 1391678

rows.plain_value:
Issue: When we ran the SharePoint imports it was importing SharePoint Items which was a huuuuugggggeeee volume of data

In order to filter it , we applied the filter as below:
\{
"SharePoint Group": \{},
"SharePoint Site Collection": \{},
"SharePoint Site": \{},
"SharePoint List": \{},
"SharePoint Folder": \{},
"SharePoint File": \{}
}

And referred to https://saviynt.freshdesk.com/support/solutions/articles/43000518019-microsoft-office-365-connector-guide as well to apply the filters

This option above filter folder and file level data - which is required for us.

Please advise how to proceed

Fresh Desk Ticket # 1421469

rows.plain_value:
I know we an option to send job alerts when a job errors out but it would be nice to have an option to send alerts while a job is hung/stuck. Have some sort of threshold value where in a job should not run more that typically 120 mins if not send out an alert.


This causes a lot of problems when other jobs cannot run unless we preform a DB restart. It is important for us to get alerts when such kind of scenarios occur

Sandipto Banerjee Jul 11, 2022
Thank you for providing this feedback. I will double check with the team if we have a Timeout setting and a corresponding notification.

Saurabh Agrahari Jul 13, 2022
Team, this requirement can be fulfilled by analytics and control central as well. you can schedule and send an email with details of all the hung jobs.

floyd.smith@mckesson.com Jul 25, 2022
An IGA tool should be robust enough to provide appropriate alerting when job failure occurs. This should be an out of box feature that requires little to no actions from a customer standpoint.

Abhishek Nair Jul 26, 2022
The analytics and Control center reports are out of the box but is configurable. The main issue here i how do we identify if the job is HUNG or just taking more time to process.

floyd.smith@mckesson.com Aug 8, 2022
@Saurabh Agrahari how do you achieve this level of monitoring and alerting via analytics? Who or what team from Saviynt can assist McKesson with this action?

rvartak@simeio.com Aug 8, 2022
You can use threshold in analytics Query & get difference between job start date vs job end date

cgerow@slb.com Aug 8, 2022
We had the same issue and achieved it by doing the below.

1 - create an email template to email the support team


2 - create an analytics report to email with attachment with above email template.

Modify the sql below to get your data by hours or days. We run ours daily. This is for Failed jobs but you can modify the SQL for jobendate is null and jobstartdate is within 2 hours of todays hours etc.

Select jobid, externalconnection, jobstartdate, jobenddate, jobname, savresponse, jobgroup, triggername, triggertype from ecmimportjob where datediff(jobenddate,current_date()) >= -1 and savresponse != 'Success'


3 - schedule the report to run per #2


We also created one for Failed Tasks in case your interested. Modify as needed

select at.status as 'StatusKey', at.taskkey, accountname as 'GIN', u.email, ep.endpointname, ev.displayname, at.startdate,provisioningcomments from arstasks at left join entitlement_values ev on at.entitlement_valuekey = ev.entitlement_valuekey inner join users u on at.accountname = u.username inner join endpoints ep on ep.endpointkey = at.endpoint

where at.status in (8,3) and provisioningcomments like("%REJECTED%") and datediff(at.startdate,current_date()) >= -1

or at.status in (8,3) and provisioningcomments like("%Failed%") and datediff(at.startdate,current_date()) >= -1

or provisioningcomments like("%REJECTED%") and datediff(at.startdate,current_date()) >= -1

or provisioningcomments like("%Failed%") and datediff(at.startdate,current_date()) >= -1

ryne.gervais@osaic.com Dec 29, 2023
I have a similar Idea that should go along with this. Along side reporting, there should be a way for the customer (Admins) to terminate these hung jobs without needing to create a support ticket or restart the whole environment. Especially in a production situation during the middle of a work day, this is a horrible option.

Something as simple as a UI option to at least try to terminate the long running jobs should be available as a first option, and then restarting the whole environment would be an escalation - last resort.

rvartak@simeio.com Dec 29, 2023
It's already approved https://ideas.saviynt.com/ideas/EIC-I-3349

Sai Sruthin Charan Tirunahari May 16, 2025
This is released: https://docs.saviyntcloud.com/bundle/EIC-Admin-25/page/Content/Chapter10-Job-Control-Panel/Terminating-Jobs.htm

cgerow@slb.com May 19, 2025
That link is only for 2 jobs. We need that same option for all jobs. "Starting with Amsterdam GA release, you can now immediately terminate long-running or unresponsive SOD Evaluation (RiskSODEvaluationJob) and JRMRECOMMENTATIONJOB jobs."

When trying to filter the tasks in Home page, we are unable to filter them based on the task type or submit date or the status of the task. Please include the following columns in the Advanced search of pending/completed tasks.


1. Task Type
2. Parent Task
3. Entitlement
4. Request ID
5. Owner
6. Creation Date
7. Status
8. Update User
9. Task Update Date

Today, we can define an email template when a task completes but there is no distinction between a successful provisioning event and a failure.

Would add that the error message of these failures are also not easy to interpret or shared....how can we improve to bucket these errors with better observability....

Saurabh Agrahari Jul 29, 2022
Thanks for reaching out. One way to achieve this use case would be to utilize control centre. We can create a control for all the failed task and give a holistic view from there. The email notification also can be derived form control center.

chris.vermilya@fishtech.group Jul 29, 2022
Saurabh, can you have control center send an email to the user and requestor, notifying them that the provisioning failed for their request?

Saurabh Agrahari Aug 2, 2022
You can do it using Analytics. Analytics has a feature to send email notification to the users mentioned in every row. You have certain syntax to follow where a column alias should be username other columns could give task details. And Analytics will auto send the email for every row. You should be able to locate an article on freshdesk with details of this feature.

rows.plain_value:
Marriott Active Directory has multiple domains. The requirement is to allow inter-domain group provisioning which can be achieved only by using an ADSI connection. Which is why we had moved from AD to ADSI connection.

Now, with ADSI connection in place, there is a requirement to automate OU creation in AD. The current ADSI connection does not have the CREATEORGJSON or UPDATEORGJSON to handle this capability.


Regards,

Sneha

rows.plain_value: We have a requirement is to generate region specific unique samAccountName by validating from AD. The region specific combinations required to be till 999. Adding incremental number manually for all regions doesn't look a clean configuration. Thus need to have INCREMENTINDEX operation supported in AD connector's CHECKFORUNIQUE json.

rows.plain_value: Currently, when a job is run manually from the Job Control Panel, when you view the Job History Details, the system does not provide any audit trail of who ran the job. The Update User is available, but that only shows who last modified or made changes to the job. If a user takes a manual step, within the system, to run a job, their ID should be captured as part of the Job History Details. Jobs can be run to perform maintenance on the system, provision entitlements, terminate users, and other high risk functions. If someone is taking steps to manually process those, outside of scheduled workflows, that must be captured for basic audit trails, tracking, and troubleshooting. The fact that Saviynt isn't capturing manual actions taken by users is a major gap, regardless of whether the job code has been reviewed and approved. There should be no assumption made the user has approval to run it outside of normally scheduled times.

rows.plain_value:
Office 365 is retiring Basic Auth for IMAP. This appears to be used by Saviynt. We do utilize the Email eater using IMAP.

We attempted to configure the OAUTH connector but could not get it to work as expected. See ticket #217881

Current OAUTH email eater only pulls back 10 emails at a time. We have thousands of emails waiting to be processed and it can never keep up. We need to be able to configure this setting or expand it to pull all unread emails like the basic auth IMAP does?


Need to make this setting configurable or modify it to read more than 10 unread emails at a time.

rows.plain_value:
MAX_CHANGENUMBER in LDAP Connector should not get updated in the event there is a reconciliation failure.

As of today, we have scenarios in a client implementation where the MAX_CHANGENUMBER gets updated even when the recon fails in between. Due to which we have data losses. Considering the amount of data client has, the full recon is done only once a month. Hence this is mitigated via custom queries as of today. We need an improvement in the connector where MAX_CHANGENUMBer is updated only in the event of successful recon in the LDAP connections so the incremental recon is accurate.

rows.plain_value: MAX_CHANGENUMBER in LDAP Connector should not get updated in the event there is a reconciliation failure
rows.html_value: MAX_CHANGENUMBER in LDAP Connector should not get updated in the event there is a reconciliation failure
rows.rich_value: MAX_CHANGENUMBER in LDAP Connector should not get updated in the event there is a reconciliation failure

rows.plain_value:
Rajiv Kumar Oct 18, 2022
Hi Vikas,

Pls add some more details around what is leading to the recon failures. Is this more of a transient issue or an issue that may need more review.

Shiva Vuppala Oct 18, 2022
For example, If we have set the page_size as 100, the MAX_CHANGENUMBER on the connection is updated under externalconnattvalue after every fetch. The users data is updated under users table only after completing the Preprocessing. In case if the Import Job fails after the fetching the data at anytime before updating/inserting the records in users table. We'll have the latest MAX_CHANGENUMBER already updated which results in data loss when the next job executes will start fetching the records from the MAX_CHANGENUMBER in the connection.

It is recommended to update the MAX_CHANGENUMBER in the connection only if the Import Job is successful.

Vikas Jain Oct 18, 2022
irrespective of the error caused, max change ntnumber cannot be a reliable counter for incremental imports if the number is being stored even during an error occurence. this causes a loss of data during incremental which is especially a problem in a large client like IBM where incremental imports are heavily relied for daily recon. So this becomes critical.

As of today there are customizations just to ensure the max_changenumber is changed back to a pre-error state in case an error occurs. This needs to be standardized in the connector.

Shiva and I would be happy to discuss this in detail with you.

Vikas Jain Nov 3, 2022
Rajiv,


let me know if you would like to meet with Shiva and I to gather more feedback on this enhancement. This feels like a critical platform enhancement.


Thanks!

Rajiv Kumar Nov 28, 2024
Greetings,

This idea is closed as the corresponding issue has been fixed.

rows.rich_value: • Workflow

rows.plain_value:
There are certain Workflows which are generic and comonly used across many client . These workflows can be available to be used as OOB workflows . Which will help client to quickly onboard the applications and reach Fast90 target or make a copy of this workflow and enhance it to address any specific need

1. 1st level approver with or without escalation ( Manager )
2. Two level approver with or without escalation ( Manager & level 2 Manager )
3. Two level - App owner approval with or without escalation ( Manager , followed by Application Owner )
4. Two level - entitlement owner approval ( Manager , entitlement owner )
5. Two level - Role owner ( Manager , Role Owner )
6. Advance workflow 1 : Addressing Complex scenario 1 on how to access the entitlement and hierarchy attributes
7. Advance workflow 2 : Addressing complex scenario 2 on how to access account owner , its existing access information
8. Advance workflow 3 : addressing scenario on how to access information around Request ,Service accounts , security system , endpoint
9. Advance workflow 4 : Addressing scenario on the last update on the record ( account or user )
10. Advance workflow 5 : Addressing scenario around how to access the attributes related to CPAM integration and session

rows.plain_value:
Hi Team,

I believe it would help optimize speed for reconciliation, if it was possible to further filter on the entitlements such as a sub-group of entitlements for AADGroups where needed. An enterprise that has an extremely large number of AADGroups, let's say replicated from Active Directory. We would want to not manage these groups via the AADConnector rather from the AD Connector and so these groups should be excluded from reconciliation.

rows.plain_value:
Request to modify STATUS_THRESHOLD_CONFIG in the connection so that we can have a different configuration for different endpoints in the same connection.


For Example - in the EDS connection, if we have different STATUS_THRESHOLD_CONFIG for endpoints such as MGS and Opera PMS by applying conditional statements that would help us meet major requirements.

Greetings,

Status and threshold is intended for mapping of status and defining thresholds for pausing updates in the case of an error condition. Both of these are application connectivity specific and hence will be present on the connection object. This idea will be closed.

rows.plain_value:
an the threshold be set up as a dynamic value instead of a static one in connection for import in order to prevent account / entitlement losses?


Use cases when the static value isnt a fit:


- there are multiple PGM applications using same connection for recon, some applications have a little as 20 accounts, some of the applications have thousands of accounts


- eventually account count might change over time and the static value wont be reflecting correct number



Can you please create a jira regarding this enhancement to use some dynamic value or let us set up some % of total accounts per sec system?


FD Ticket : 455184
